user.title: Source entities
user.description: Select the entities SEMS should read. SEMS never fetches data itself; it only reads these existing sensors.

data_description.price_entity: Using the core Nord Pool integration? Pick any of its sensors — which one doesn't matter, SEMS fetches the hourly prices itself. Using HACS Nord Pool, EnergyZero, Frank Energie or similar? Pick the specific sensor that has the hourly prices in its attributes (raw_today or prices — check in Developer tools → States).
data_description.pv_forecast_entity: Pick any sensor of your solar forecast integration (Forecast.Solar, Solcast, ...) — which one doesn't matter, SEMS asks the integration for its hourly forecast the same way the Energy dashboard does. Leave empty if you have no solar panels.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: The defaults work out of the box, but the closer these values match your energy contract, the more accurate the scores. Everything can be changed later via Configure.

data.price_type: What kind of price does the source entity provide?
data.pause_max_consecutive_hours: Longest pause allowed in a row

data_description.price_type: All-in: the price already includes taxes and VAT — most supplier integrations (EnergyZero, Zonneplan, Frank Energie) work like this. Raw: the bare market price (core Nord Pool). The next screen asks for your taxes in both cases.
data_description.resolution: Hour blocks (default) suit devices that cannot switch quickly, like heat pumps. Quarter-hour blocks follow the 15-minute prices some suppliers already use; rank then runs 1-96 instead of 1-24.
data_description.export_fee: What your supplier charges per exported kWh ('terugleverkosten'). Enter as a positive number — SEMS subtracts it from the market price to calculate what exporting earns.
data_description.pv_capacity: Total rated power of your panels in Watts, e.g. 12 panels × 405 Wp = 4860. Used to estimate how much of your consumption your own solar power covers per hour. Real output rarely reaches this rated maximum and that is fine — it makes the estimate slightly conservative. 0 = automatic: assume the sunniest hour of the day covers your consumption.
data_description.price_free_threshold: Below this all-in price, power counts as free: the score goes above 100 and the free-power sensor turns on.
data_description.pause_hours: Marks the worst hours of each day for switching a device OFF - a freezer, a boiler - via binary_sensor.sems_pause_now. 0 keeps that sensor off. Expensive hours cluster, so SEMS does not simply take the worst hours in a row; it spreads them using the limit below.
data_description.pause_max_consecutive_hours: How long the device may stay off in one stretch. 1 hour means every pause is followed by at least an hour on, so a freezer never thaws. The limit also holds across midnight. Costs almost nothing: on real price curves spreading the pauses gives up well under a percent versus taking the worst hours back to back.
data_description.debug_mode: Adds sensor.sems_diagnostics showing exactly what data SEMS found, so you can verify everything works. Turn off once you trust the numbers.

taxes.title: Taxes and markup
taxes.description: These values matter for every price type. Raw market prices: SEMS adds them on top to calculate what you actually pay. All-in prices: SEMS uses them in reverse, to estimate the bare market price inside your price — that determines what exporting your solar power earns. Please fill them in as accurately as you can (check your energy contract); the defaults are Dutch 2026 values and work fine to start with. Enter all amounts as positive numbers.

data_description.supplier_markup: What your supplier charges per kWh on top of the market price. Enter as a positive number.
data_description.energy_tax: The energy tax per kWh (Dutch 'energiebelasting'). Default is the Dutch 2026 value.
data_description.vat_percent: The VAT percentage applied to price, markup and tax.

data.price_type: What kind of price does the source entity provide?
data.pause_max_consecutive_hours: Longest pause allowed in a row

options.raw: Raw market price (SEMS adds taxes and VAT)
